description: US public support for marijuana legalization continued its decades-long climb from ~60% (2018) to ~68% (2020), crossing the two-thirds threshold. State-level legalization votes (Michigan 2018, Illinois 2019, multiple states 2020) reinforced the trend. Support was broad-based across demographics except 65+.

key_events.description: Gallup 2018: 66% support legalization, highest ever at that point. Canada fully legalizes marijuana (October 2018), providing international demonstration effect.
Michigan legalizes recreational use via ballot (November 2018, 56%). Illinois becomes first state to legalize via legislature (June 2019). Normalization accelerates.
House passes MORE Act (December 2020) to decriminalize federally. First time either chamber passes such legislation. Bipartisan shift visible.
November 2020: Arizona, Montana, New Jersey, South Dakota all pass legalization ballot measures. Red-state victories (Montana, South Dakota) signal broad cultural shift.
Gallup 2020: 68% support. Pew: 91% support at least medical use. 15 states + DC fully legal. Tax revenue data ($10B+ nationally) provides economic argument. Support plateaus at supermajority.

calibration_notes: Slow, steady, monotonic increase reflecting generational replacement and normalization. Gallup tracked this from 25% (1995) to 68% (2020). The 2018-2020 window shows the mature phase of this S-curve. Very few undecideds — most Americans had formed opinions. Remaining opposition concentrated in 65+ demographic and evangelical Christians. Tests: glacial opinion shift, generational replacement, demonstration effects from state labs, normalization dynamics, ceiling effects.
